# LinkedIn — Honors & Awards

## 1. Outstanding Mentor Award - AI Hackathon Festival 2025

**Associated with:** She Sharp
**Issuer:** AI Forum NZ, She Sharp, and Auckland University of Technology
**Issue date:** Aug 2025
**Media:** `Technical guidance to Westlake Girls High School students` · `1755253595927.jpeg`

```
Named an Outstanding Mentor at the AI Hackathon Festival 2025 — a two-day event co-organised by AI Forum NZ, She Sharp, and Auckland University of Technology that challenged teams to build AI solutions aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

Served as one of 14 expert mentors guiding 11 teams and 80+ participants through the full build, from idea to working prototype inside a 24-hour window. The participants ranged from Westlake Girls High School students to working professionals across multiple industries.

What the mentoring covered:
• Helping high-school students use AI tools effectively and understand where they genuinely help
• Guiding adult competitors in turning a rough concept into a functional Minimum Viable Product under time pressure
• Hands-on support across AI/ML implementation, full-stack engineering, and system architecture
• Ethical-AI considerations and user-centred design, so the solutions were responsible as well as working

Worked alongside teams tackling real problems — food insecurity, digital accessibility, workforce upskilling, and indigenous environmental stewardship — giving each the technical depth to ship something demonstrable by the deadline.
```

## 2. Outstanding Performer — UN Women FemTech Hackathon 2025

**Associated with:** Sanicle
**Issuer:** FemTech Weekend
**Issue date:** Mar 2025
**Media:** `20250313155203_FemTech_Sanicle.jpg`

```
Recognised as an Outstanding Performer at the 2025 UN Women FemTech Hackathon in Beijing — an event focused on empowering women's workplace health, run in collaboration with Sanicle and the NGO CSW Forum.

Competed as an independent developer, contributing innovative solutions to women's health challenges in professional environments and standing out in a field built around team entries. The recognition reflected both the technical quality of the build and its real-world fit for the workplace-health brief that the hackathon set.
```

## 3. UN CSW 69 Speaker — Beyond Beijing 30 Conference

**Associated with:** Sanicle
**Issuer:** United Nations Headquarters, New York
**Issue date:** Mar 2025
**Links:** <https://www.unwomen.org/en/how-we-work/commission-on-the-status-of-women> · <https://www.unwomen.org/en/how-we-work/commission-on-the-status-of-women/csw69-2025>
**Media:** `Commission on the Status of Women` · `CSW69 / Beijing+30 (2025)` · `I gave an online speech at the United Nations Headquarters in New York`  _(Show all 6 media)_

```
Invited as a featured speaker to present Sanicle AI — a women's workplace-health management platform — at the UN CSW 69 conference marking the 30th anniversary of the Beijing Declaration, held at United Nations Headquarters in New York on 14 March 2025.

Presented to an audience of international delegates, UN officials, and global leaders, demonstrating how AI can address workplace gender inequities through practical, privacy-respecting health solutions rather than policy alone.

The presentation drew direct response from senior figures:
• IBM representatives expressed interest in a pilot collaboration
• The Minister of Gender and Children's Affairs from Sierra Leone praised the platform as the first comprehensive solution connecting menstrual-cycle data to actionable workplace policy through AI analysis
• Multiple NGOs and member-state representatives followed up with collaboration requests

Speaking at a UN forum of this stature placed an independently built FemTech platform in front of the people who shape global gender-equity policy — and turned a technical demo into concrete partnership interest.
```

## 4. Dean's List - Top 5% Academic Performance

**Associated with:** Lincoln University (NZ)
**Issuer:** Lincoln University, Faculty of Environment, Society & Design
**Issue date:** Jan 2025
**Links:** <https://www.lincoln.ac.nz/>
**Media:** `Congratulatory Email`

```
Named to the Dean's List for academic excellence in Semester 2, 2024, placing among the top 5% of students in the Faculty of Environment, Society & Design at Lincoln University — New Zealand's specialist land-based university.

The recognition came during the Master of Applied Computing programme, alongside full-time engineering work — evidence of sustained academic performance maintained in parallel with shipping production software.
```

## 5. Excellence Award — FemTech China Women's Health Technology Challenge

**Associated with:** Lincoln University (NZ)
**Issuer:** FemTech China
**Issue date:** Dec 2024
**Links:** <https://femtechcn.com/>
**Media:** `HONOR CERTIFICATE` · `FemTech China`

```
Won the Excellence Award at China's inaugural FemTech Weekend Women's Health Technology Challenge as a core member of Team BuXun.

Run in partnership with C2C Startups, the competition set out to advance technological innovation in women's healthcare. Led product development through intensive workshops, mentoring sessions, and collaborative team work, culminating in a complete pitch deck and prototype addressing a specific women's-health challenge.

Entries were judged on product potential, technical innovation, commercial viability, and team collaboration by a panel of industry experts and Chengdu Regional Science and Technology Consulting Co., Ltd.

This is the same FemTech Weekend organisation later joined as CTO in March 2025 — the award preceded and directly informed that subsequent leadership engagement.
```

## 6. Master of Applied Computing with Distinction

**Associated with:** Lincoln University (NZ)
**Issuer:** Lincoln University (Te Whare Wānaka o Aoraki)
**Issue date:** Dec 2024
**Links:** <https://www.lincoln.ac.nz/>
**Media:** `Congratulatory Email` · `image001.png` · `image002.png`  _(Show all 4 media)_

```
Awarded Distinction for the Master of Applied Computing at Lincoln University — New Zealand's specialist land-based university — for achieving an average of 80% or above across every course in the programme.

Distinction is the programme's highest classification, marking consistent top-tier performance across advanced computing coursework and research rather than a single strong result.
```
